guest: Rick (CFO background, HBS, co-founder Tickle) and Jeff (Yahoo/product operator, runs Grindr)

business_ideas.idea: Private equity acquisition of profitable mid-market companies with cash-flowing operations
Sourcing stable portfolio companies from underperforming VC portfolio companies that won't venture scale
Bundling small-to-medium profitable companies together (IAC model) with centralized operations
Purchasing bookkeeping business and applying AI to improve operations and customer experience
Acquiring commercial brokerage business and upgrading technology stack
AI applications in legal, medical, and engineering sectors where documentation is high
Crypto-focused ventures and disruption plays in legacy industries

predictions.text: AI will transform legal, medical, and engineering sectors through automation and documentation leverage
Next decade will generate more wealth/disruption despite current bubble concerns; winners will be clear by 2035

dynamics: Rick drives financial/risk analysis; Jeff drives product and culture narrative. Sam and Shaan are fascinated, asking deep questions about PE dealmaking vs. startup life. Episode shifts from Grindr turnaround story to practical PE playbook, ending with future opportunity thesis. High respect between parties; guests reveal philosophical preference for PE over startup grind.
frameworks: PE Deal Sourcing Triangle — network deals, VC portfolio opportunities, bundling strategies
Risk Mitigation Framework — private equity focuses on downside protection vs. venture's portfolio math
Barbell Thesis — mix small portfolio companies (passive board) + large full-operator plays (Grindr model)
Playbook Transfer — applying proven patterns (Tinder pricing/UX) to comparable businesses
SPV Model (Special Purpose Vehicle) — assembling domain experts and capital partners for single deals
learnings: Homophobic market bias against Grindr created valuation arbitrage; institutional gatekeeping can create founder opportunity
Most founders overestimate valuations based on historical SaaS multiples; private equity uses EBITDA multiples for cash-flowing consumer businesses
Talent quality (and cultural turnaround) can unlock 2-3x revenue growth without new product innovation

banger_quotes.quote: In angel investing you're playing odds on one-in-ten; private equity you're a chief risk officer
You either get lightning in a bottle or zero; private equity's the middle path with control
Grindr saves more lives through sexual education than Tinder ever will

numbers_dropped: Grindr EBITDA at purchase: $45 million (12-13x multiple)
Grindr App Store rating: 1.8 stars (0.8 vs. minimum)
Grindr revenue at IPO: $200 million (doubled in 2.5 years)
notable: Rare deep dive into PE playbook by two non-traditional operators; Grindr story reveals how cultural bias created market inefficiency. Hosts shift from pure entrepreneur mentality to appreciating PE's risk-controlled alternative path.
